channel.title: Blog on Kuldeep Chowhan
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Kuldeep Chowhan

item.title: AI Agents for Normal Builders: A Practical Guide to Getting Real Work Done
item.description: A practical guide to building AI agents that actually ship. Covers agent maturity levels, bounded agency, real-world use cases, Claude Code examples, and a launch checklist for builders who want agents that do real work.

item.title: What Platform Engineering Actually Means in 2026
item.description: Platform engineering has evolved from a buzzword into a core discipline. Here's what it actually means in 2026, based on lessons from building platforms at Walmart, Expedia, and SoFi.

AI Agents for Normal Builders: A Practical Guide to Getting Real Work Done

AI agents are having a moment. Most of the conversation is either hype or doom. Neither helps you ship.

An AI agent is not magic. It is a workflow.

If you strip it down, an agent is simply: LLM + tools + memory + a loop + guardrails.

When that combination is designed well, it compresses 30-to-90-minute tasks into 3-to-10 minutes of review. When designed poorly, it becomes a confident intern that breaks production.
